headline: Software Firm Medallia Is a Problem for Private Credit. Blackstone, Thoma Bravo Have Exposure.
summary: In the $1 trillion private credit market, investment firms like Blackstone,  KKR Apollo Global Management Ares Management  and  Blue Owl  make high-rate loans of around 10% to junk-grade companies, mostly to finance leveraged buyouts.  Medallia, which provides customer surveys to corporate clients, hasn’t performed well financially since it was taken private by Thoma Bravo for $6.4 billion in 2021.

headline: Private Credit Squeeze: Billions Trapped as Investors Rush for Exit
summary: ARES, APO, BLK, BX and OWL face pressure as $13B in private credit exits strain liquidity, leaving $4.6B trapped and exposing cracks in the $2T market.

raw.headline: Private Credit Squeeze: Billions Trapped as Investors Rush for Exit
raw.summary: ARES, APO, BLK, BX and OWL face pressure as $13B in private credit exits strain liquidity, leaving $4.6B trapped and exposing cracks in the $2T market.

headline: Apollo Faces Liquidity Strain While Pursuing Japan Deal And Board Changes
summary: Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) has imposed withdrawal limits on certain private credit funds following elevated investor redemption requests. The firm has agreed to acquire Nippon Sheet Glass in what it describes as its largest private equity investment to date in Japan. Apollo announced the passing of board member David Simon, raising questions around upcoming board and governance changes. Apollo, a large alternative asset manager with a significant private credit franchise, now sits...
